methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.5B - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Abridge is a Series E biotech & health company currently valued at $5.3B post-money, having raised $1.2B+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 companies: 3 public-market comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the same sector and valuation tier, including Tempus AI ($6.0B), Ultima Genomics ($4.0B), Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Freenome ($2.6B), and Insitro ($2.4B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression anchors to Abridge's $5.3B last post-money and applies a typical Series E-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.4x, implying $7.4B. Peer-median analysis across the 10 same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $2.3B, with a range spanning $1.5B to $6.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples cannot be applied, as private company revenue is not on file. The public Biotech & Health sector median trades at 7.4x sales across three tickers, but without Abridge's revenue figure, this method is inapplicable.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.0B (low) to $8.5B (high), with a median of $4.9B. The current $5.3B valuation sits comfortably within this envelope, though notably above the peer cohort median of $2.3B. Key uncertainty centers on revenue disclosure and trajectory relative to stage peers, which would anchor a revenue-multiple cross-check and clarify whether the premium reflects genuine operational divergence or market-cycle timing.
